# Extracao: transcription_mp4_13__Trait_12_-_Great_Salespeople_do_not_view_failed_sales_as_lost_sales_

- **Fonte:** transcription_mp4_13__Trait_12_-_Great_Salespeople_do_not_view_failed_sales_as_lost_sales_.txt
- **Tipo:** TXT

---

Great salespeople do not view failed sales as lost sales. They actually see a failed sale, a lost sale, you didn't get it, as an investment in the future. They don't calculate, oh I made four sales in this much money. They calculate like this. I made farty calls and made this much money. You see the difference? See they see the other 36 that didn't actually fund them as being part of their funding process in their business building. Look, great salespeople don't equate deals, the deal that don't close or the deals that do close as part of their paycheck. They see all deals as part of the process is filling their pipeline. Every failed sale should be viewed as an investment in building your business. Great salespeople see all of them as paying, not just some of them. They're flat, completely flat, non-emotional on this lost sale. They don't like losing a sale, but they have no emotion about it. They know it's part of their business building process.
